{"key_event":"War of the Schools and secular education reform","reasoning":"Civil society remained relatively strong through party, clerical, regional, educational, and commercial mobilization, though polarization and civil war fragmented national collective capacity. State power rose only modestly: secular schooling, legal standardization, telegraphs/commerce, and Liberal military victory expanded central reach, but radical federalism still left taxation, militias, elections, and administration largely in sovereign-state hands.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":6.8,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":3.1}
